title: sideroxylon spathulatum var. molokaiense
intro: Sideroxylon spathulatum var. molokaiense, also known as the Molokai ironwood or Molokai stiltroot, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae that is native to the island of Molokai in Hawaii. This plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, infections, and other health issues. The health benefits of Sideroxylon spathulatum var. molokaiense include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
